Name: Repealing section 19-163.2 of such code, relating to limiting the use of adhesive stickers on motor vehicles to enforce alternate side parking rules.
Title: A Local Law to amend the administrative code of the city of New York, in relation to repealing section 19-163.2 of such code, relating to limiting the use of adhesive stickers on motor vehicles to enforce alternate side parking rules
BodyName: Committee on Sanitation and Solid Waste Management

History.Description: This Introduction was Introduced by Council
This Introduction was Referred to Comm by Council to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure
This Introduction was Re-referred to Committee by Council to the Committee on Sanitation and Solid Waste Management
This Introduction was Hearing Held by Committee
History.BodyName: Committee on Sanitation and Solid Waste Management

History.Description: This Introduction was Laid Over by Committee
History.BodyName: Committee on Sanitation and Solid Waste Management

Summary: This bill would repeal the 2012 local law that barred the New York City Department of Transportation (DOT) and Department of Sanitation (DSNY) from placing stickers on motor vehicles as a method of enforcing alternate side parking rules.
Text:
Be it enacted by the Council as follows:
      
      
      Section 1. Section 19-163.2 of the administrative code of the city of New York, as added by local law number 20 for the year 2012, is amended to read as follows:
      � 19-163.2 [Limitation on the use] Use of adhesive stickers in the enforcement of alternate side of the street parking rules. [Neither the] The department [nor], the department of sanitation, [shall] and any other department designated by the department of sanitation may affix an adhesive sticker to [any] a window of a motor vehicle [solely in connection with] to advise the operator of such vehicle of the [enforcement] violation by such operator of alternate side of the street parking rules. The dimension of any such sticker shall not exceed eight and one-half inches by 11 inches.
      � 2. This local law takes effect immediately.


NAW/MBB
LS #19711
Int. #1334-2025
06/18/2026 3:20 PM
